pattern: A base raised to a huge digit-array exponent is reduced modulo a constant by walking the digits Horner-style: each step raises the running result to the tenth power and multiplies in the base raised to the next digit, all under the modulus.
hints: You can never form the whole exponent as one integer, but you can process its decimal digits one at a time from the most significant.
Moving from the digits seen so far to including one more digit means the exponent gets multiplied by ten and then increased by that digit.
Keep a running answer; for each digit d set answer to answer raised to the tenth power times the base raised to d, every multiplication taken modulo 1337.

edge_inputs.description: two-digit exponent crosses the Horner step
base one stays one regardless of exponent
base shares a factor with the modulus
large base must be reduced before powering
base equal to the modulus collapses to zero

stress.description: random base and a long digit array
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    a = rng.randint(1, 2147483647)
    length = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    b = [rng.randint(1, 9)] + [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(length - 1)]
    return [a, b]
stress.note: first digit nonzero to respect the no-leading-zero rule; long arrays exercise the digit walk
